programme.desc: Georgie chooses to remain in the UK in a training role as 2 Section prepares to return to Afghanistan, sending a trainee medic in her place. However, when her protegee's confidence slips in the face of the challenges she faces, Georgie feels compelled to join the new recruits in need of her support. However, while protecting a local doctor-turned-politician, she is thrust into an encounter with the militant leader that killed Elvis(n)

programme.title: My Left Nut
programme.desc: Drama about a 15-year-old boy who is horrified to discover a swelling on his testicle. Unprepared to deal with his embarrassing problem and unable to talk to his widowed mother about it, he decides to ignore it, fearing that his friends will make fun of him and that the growth will jeopardise his chances of going out with the girl he has a crush on. Starring Nathan Quinn-O'Rawe(n)

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Anita Rani looks at problems caused by single-use toys, while the residents of the street in Bristol attempt to go plastic free during Christmas - a period that generates a 125,000 ton of packaging. Plus, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall reveals the plastic industry's plans to increase production by 50 per cent before 2040(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: Disturbing secrets are exposed about a man struck down on his stag night, and Billie is tempted to act rashly. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Glynis Barber(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports live from a specialist police rehab unit in Berkshire, and Rav Wilding finds out about the lengths farmers will go to protect animals(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a semi-detached in Sittingbourne, Kent, a cottage in Landrake in Cornwall and a flat in London's Maida Vale(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An opportunist pretending to be a number of different people is caught out, and surveillance footage uncovers a driver fraudulently claiming disability for a serious back injury(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross challenges two teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to scour two antique centres and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in Norfolk. Experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young offer support, while away from the action Charlie sees a selection of of rare toys(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi's prison ordeal finally comes to an end as The Mill prepares for a special day. Meanwhile, Ruhma is drawn into a shoplifting incident with a unexpected twist(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: Infidelity leads to a man being found dead in bed and a woman being menaced. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to Suffolk to help a couple looking to set down roots in the countryside with a budget of £900,000, and he also meets a dynamic dairy farming family(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the skips at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for items to restore and sell for a profit. Items include a pair of leather wallets and a rocking chair(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Mother and son duo Pat and Tommy compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus Daily Update: BBC News Special
programme.desc: The latest reports about Covid-19. Presented by Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Geoff Clark from Sunderland hopes that audio expert Mark Stuckey can restore a beloved 1960s jukebox, and horologist Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a 400-year-old wall clock that has laid broken in a box for years. Elsewhere, Tim Gunn restores a stowaway bike to its former glory(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as the last group of six hopefuls compete in the culinary contest. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The others must come up with a dish using pigeon, beetroot and bacon in the Three Ingredients Challenge, after which two cooks are sent home, leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for three former contestants(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley experiences the pressure of life on the frontline of mental health services, spending three weeks working alongside staff at Springfield Hospital in London. During her investigation, she sees first hand how tough it is for medical staff to help young people who are struggling with their mental health, and finds out how hard it is make decisions on what is best for them when their lives are on the line(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: The second series of the spy thriller picks up where the first left off, with MI5 agent Eve and assassin Villanelle left reeling from their final devastating encounter. Eve finds herself summoned back to London by Carolyn, who offers her a new opportunity. Villanelle is forced to go on the run, desperate to avoid the wrath of her former employers and plotting to follow her enemy back to London(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Colleagues Dean and Julian are paired with chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie, with fish likely to be on the menu as both work for a charity supporting fishing communities. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists prepare for another two challenges but first there's a surprise as, for one week only, Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge joins the judges in Angela Hartnett's place. Then it's down to business as Mary Berry asks them to cook their ultimate family pie and Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish using a root vegetable usually served on the side. As usual, those who least impress the judges compete in the eliminator, a recipe close to Tom's heart that he expects to be executed perfectly. Presented by Claudia Winkleman(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament presented by Alicia McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores some of western Europe's most dramatic shorelines, telling stories, investigating notable landmarks and exploring mysteries that have played an important role in the region's development(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Christina Trevanion search for items at a West Sussex car boot sale with a budget of £250 each, with Mark battling broken bones to make a profit(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation, abandoning the cab for a punt along the River Cherwell in Oxford. Terry visits the city's old jail and they investigate the origins of the Oxford sausage and the famous old Oxford marmalade factory, before meeting students at the university who have formed a society to assess the scientific benefits of pizza(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Bridget takes charge of a dangerous situation, a betrayed Patricia publicly confronts Tom Callaghan, and Johno's methods are questioned(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: Journalist Tom Dyckhoff presents a competition searching for untapped interior design talent in Britain, with experts Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood acting as judges. In each heat, three hopefuls take on similar rooms in neighbouring homes and have 48 hours, £1,000 and the help of a builder to interpret and realise the homeowners' wishes(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Weather presenter Carol Kirkwood joins Brian Conley to revisit favourite TV memories, including Starsky and Hutch, The Liver Birds and Play School(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Five experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, with David Harper excited by an early device for watching films, while David Fletcher and James Lewis reminisce about Rupert the Bear books and Dinky toys. Paul Martin enjoys a ride in a tank on historic former military camp(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kelly Cates, Tyger Drew-Honey, Desiree Burch and Matt Allwright go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea(n)

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose sets off from Ladram Bay, heading for the chalk cliffs of Beer Head in South Devon. Here John Scott takes him underground on a tour of Beer Quarry caves, where until a few years ago limestone had been extracted for almost 2,000 years. Continuing along the coastal path to Charmouth, he learns about fossil hunting and ammonites and after enjoying the wild orchids in the ancient wildflower meadows of West Hay farm, begins the steep climb up to Golden Cap, the highest point on the path with stunning views along the Jurassic Coast(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the second semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of the Canterbury Tales, the Liverpool music scene of the 1970s and ‘80s, Gregory Peck, and Pokemon, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents as the losing semi-finalists battle it out for third place, with the two teams competing to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of four students battle it out in the penultimate quarter-final, hoping to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Lesley Sharp narrates the story of a landmark legal battle by a group of women aiming to uncover the truth about birth defects in the Northamptonshire town being caused by toxic waste from the steelworks that had not been disposed of safely. The case had worldwide legal and medical ramifications as it was the first time that a court had recognised a link between airborne toxins and foetal development damage(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Northern double act Len and Tommy, more commonly known as Cheese & Crackers, last performed on stage together 30 years ago, when a fateful night at the Glasgow Pavilion drove the pair apart - seemingly for good - with Tommy walking out on Len. Now the comedians embark on one final gig together in front of an invited audience. Darkly comic drama, written by and starring The League of Gentlemen's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Sian Gibson (Peter Kay's Car Share) making a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: Documentary following Staffordshire Police officers, who talk candidly about the frustrations of being on the beat in Stoke-on-Trent, one of the UK's most deprived cities. Filmed over six months, the series captures emergency response teams trying to combat a backlog of cases, as well as a rise in gang culture, hate crime and domestic violence. Interviews with the Chief Constable and his team also reveal how they manage their limited resources, and how they feel about the decisions they have to make(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two farming families hope their much-loved pygmy goats will bring them acclaim at the Mid Devon Show, but the competition is tough and there is a record number of goats in the ring(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor and Matt Baker visit Looe in Cornwall during Cornish Pasty Week, meeting the town's pasty makers and going head to head in a competition to create their own. Margherita also finds out more about the area's seal population and the fisherman who live and work these shores. Plus, Adam Henson has his hands full as this season's lambs start to arrive, and Tom Heap discovers if the UK's leading food standards scheme is delivering when it comes to animal welfare(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: A customised 1962 Chevy Impala has arrived at the Victoria & Albert for a new exhibition dedicated to cars. But this unique vehicle has come all the way from Los Angeles and as if that move was not tough enough, Catherine Sargent is now concerned it will be a tight fit getting such a large car into the exhibition space. Curator Alice Minter has chosen a table with a micro-mosaic top as part of an exhibition to tour the world, but it will need some serious restoration work before it can go anywhere, and a rare medieval jewel undergoes a deep clean(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades enlists woodworking expert Ollie Allen to give a coffee table a makeover, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph breathes new life into a set of dining chairs(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple search for a property in Hampshire, and also visits the Mary Rose Museum to see the treasures uncovered when Henry VIII's warship was raised(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. In the final episode, the Royal National Lifeboat Institution volunteers race to rescue two teenagers stuck in mud in Cleethorpes on the Humber estuary before the incoming tide overwhelms them. Elsewhere, a team in Devon help a crew of a yacht shipwrecked on a notorious sand bar, and a routine call to pick up a boat that has broken its moorings becomes a race to save a family of four(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores nature at its most perilous on the wild waters of Britain's coast. Nick Crane reveals why the first lifeboat station was founded on the north-east coast at Bamburgh Castle, Mark Horton finds out how ferries cope with stormy seas, and Miranda Krestovnikoff explores the jellyfish of Dublin Bay - and how both swimmers and scientists are seeking an antidote to its excruciating sting. Dick Strawbridge investigates one of the strangest stories of the Second World War - did the Germans really invade the south coast in 1940?(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes a journey through the world of horticulture, getting inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters. The series progresses in alphabetical order from A to Z, with each edition featuring themes beginning with a different letter(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey are off to Belgium, where a Victorian cane topper and an acupuncture model catch their eye, but it remains to be seen which is the most valuable(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen arrive in the Roman city of Bath, where they seek out the finest delicacies, from beetroot sorbet to honey glazed dormice. A reviving brew served by the UK's reigning king of the baristas sets them up for a visit to the catacombs where an eco food initiative is making mushrooms out of waste cardboard and coffee grounds(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Patricia needs someone to do her dirty work. Medical aid for Rachel requires discovery of what drug she took, while Johno investigates Keith's links to the incident(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The final programme brings together the ultimate rules of design, room by room, featuring insider techniques for a better home used by regular judges Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood as well as contributors Oliver Heath, Orla Kiely and Kelly Hoppen. Presenter and architectural historian Tom Dyckhoff gives his own historic tour of the house - explaining how homes have shaped up over the years(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Actress Lesley Joseph, who plays viper-tongued Dorien in Birds of a Feather, joins Brian Conley to discuss how a production of David Copperfield and June Whitfield's comedy timing inspired her stage and television careers(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts David Harper and Claire Rawle search for items to sell at auction at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales. Presented by Paul Martin(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales. Along the way, he visits the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park, scene of the first Welsh victory over the All Blacks back in 1905. In Cardiff Bay, Michael discovers the departure point for one of the Edwardian era's most famous expeditions - Scott's ill-fated voyage to the Antarctic(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams set off from Panama City across the Caribbean Sea, but they face an unplanned obstacle when a state of emergency is declared in Quito, Ecuador - the destination of their next checkpoint. The teams are forced to change route in mid-race and head into the remote Colombian desert. Rob and Jen make use of night buses to get ahead, Emon and Jamiul raise some money by teaching scuba diving, and Sam struggles with the pressure and needs a break(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arden family experience corner shop life in the 1980s and 1990s, when stores changed from primary shopping destinations to a place for last minute impulse buys and were forced to make the maximum use of their floor space to stock thousands of products. The programme examines the impact of new legislation allowing supermarkets to open on Sundays, previously the only advantage small businesses had over them. ABC's Martin Fry visits the shop and reveals how the band used to use empty industrial units as cheap rehearsal space, and there is a look at the rise of the gaming industry and a revival of a famous 1980s marketing stunt(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: Staffordshire Police officers talk candidly about dealing with domestic abuse. Historically, officers have often treated domestic abuse as a private matter, but now far greater priority is given to investigating reported incidents and safeguarding potential victims. However, this change of approach has sometimes led officers to be called out to resolve petty arguments. The programme also examines the stress of being on permanent standby in the event of riots or terrorist attacks(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam is disappointed that Frankie has been missing from home for eight days without contacting her, but is relieved to be receiving messages from her daughter's friends. Dr Miller suggests she focuses on her other daughters.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The work of Liverpool's Walton Centre, the country's only specialist neurology hospital trust, as staff deal with patients suffering from the complex brain condition FND (Functional Neurological Disorder). Sixty-six-year-old Pauline has been left severely disabled by FND but there is nothing physically wrong with her body. David, a 38-year-old window cleaner, has been unable to walk more than a few steps a year. However, medical professionals are still learning about the condition - so the Walton Centre started an FND service in 2012(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Investors Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones return to those infamous hot seats as a new batch of entrepreneurs strive to seal a life-changing deal. Hopefuls include a beauty entrepreneur whose tools have a social as well as practical purpose. Two low-alcohol brewers hope the Dragons will stump up for the ultimate round of investment. Plus, the multi-millionaires try more than a business on for size when a footwear entrepreneur pitches his range of hand-finished shoes. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the second heat. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for 2016 MasterChef champion Jane Devonshire and finalists Billy Wright and Jack Layer(n)

programme.title: Being Hilary Mantel
programme.desc: Profile of author Hilary Mantel in the six months leading up to the publication of The Mirror and the Light, the final novel in her award-winning Tudor trilogy telling the story of the extraordinary rise and sudden fall of Thomas Cromwell. Hilary reveals why she embarked on the writing of the trilogy and how it has changed her life. She also delves into her past and how she grew up with a dark family secret(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Ellis Barrie and Mike Reid create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by two co-workers who grow their own vegetables. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey through eastern England takes him to the Snape Maltings music and arts venue, the Norfolk seaside resort of Great Yarmouth and the city of Norwich(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britain's finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane follows the Welsh coast path around Anglesey, presenting tales of inspired thinking and coastal ingenuity. Tess Dunlop reveals how a small French fishing village gave rise to the birth of British commercial radio in the 1930s, Brendan Walker investigates why the 1970 collapse of Pembrokeshire's Cleddau Bridge changed the engineering world for ever, Dick Strawbridge is in Portsmouth, where he tells of a novel First World War plan to disguise British ships by hiding them in plain sight(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey face off at an auction in Sevenoaks, where Christina plays catch-up and Mark falls in love with a romantic painting(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Mason McQueen's black cab takes Terry Wogan to the Victorian spa town of Harrogate in North Yorkshire, where they enjoy a Turkish bath and drink inordinate amounts of tea. They also sample a toffee that is actually a barley sugar, visit Agatha Christie's hide-out hotel and enjoy a slap-up dinner - with Yorkshire pudding for desert(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Johno lashes out after taking Rachel's case too personally. Meanwhile, Patricia decides whether to persist with her sordid scheme, and the Kielys are divided over their next move(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: MasterChef meets The Apprentice as Alex Polizzi takes on the challenge of finding new head chefs for restaurants that have a desperate need to fill a vacancy. Over four nights, she offers up a total of nine candidates who are put through a rigorous interview and testing process, with only the very best making it through to the final. She begins her search at The Gilpin in the Lake District, a fine dining establishment whose family owners want to find a new executive head chef who will care as much about the business as they do(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Former Blue Peter presenter and intrepid adventurer Helen Skelton joins Brian Conley for a trip down memory lane, revisiting the classic archive TV that helped shape her into the person she now is. Clips include excerpts of Newsround, the early antics of Geordie duo Ant and Dec, the extreme challenges of The Krypton Factor and children's favourite Pingu(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Culzean Castle on the west coast of Scotland in Ayrshire, experts Anita Manning and Natasha Raskin Sharp are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. A watercolour with a local connection catches Anita's eye, while Natasha is drawn to a gorgeous handwoven blanket. Paul Martin explores a hidden part of Culzean's history, uncovering the tales of smugglers working the coastline in the caves beneath the castle walls(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A volunteer in Scotland has to rescue his own father, whose fishing boat is drifting powerless towards a rocky shoreline. In Bristol, a father and son are among the crew that comes to the aid of two five-year-old girls who have drifted into the Bristol channel on an inflatable, while in Ilfracombe, lifeboat volunteers race against time to save a mother and her daughter stranded at the base of huge sea cliffs(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets sail through the Kyles of Bute to the Holy Loch and on to Ardentinny, his childhood home overlooking Loch Long. He also discovers a Viking connection in the shape of a vintage sailing boat that takes him to scale the heights of the people's mountain - The Cobbler(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Kerth Gumbs, Steve Groves, Karl O'Dell and Ivan Tisdall-Downes compete in the heats for the London and South East region. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including a dish layering textures of carrot and goats cheese inspired by Beatrix Potter, and a hazelnut dish celebrating author Julia Donaldson's acclaimed picture book, The Gruffalo. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Return of the documentary following three gifted teenagers, Liam, Kian and Jamarley, as they prepare to finish secondary school and make important decisions about their futures. Kian dreams of becoming a theoretical physicist, but has to face up to the reality of having to leave Hartlepool to pursue his dreams. In Newcastle, aspiring doctor Liam finds his work ethic is increasingly under scrutiny, while in London, Jamarley faces pressures outside school(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: Comedy following the misadventures of Leslie Winner, an eternally-optimistic yet calamitous character, as he prepares for married life. The pressure is on Leslie to find - and keep - a job to pay for his honeymoon and wedding, and to impress his disapproving father-in-law. In the opening episode, Leslie comes across an abandoned piano and comes up with a clever money-making scheme to help pay for his upcoming wedding. Starring Spencer Jones, Lucy Pearman and Shaun Williamson(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones cast their eyes over the final batch of business pitches of the current series. Stepping into the arena are an inventor who has come up with an unusual way of controlling household draughts, a businessman who claims there is money to be made in encouraging dogs to eat bugs, a fitness teacher who launches into a shimmying session, and the creator of a high-end garment bag. Evan Davis oversees proceedings as the Dragons decide if any of these pitches are worthy of investment(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: The latest international headlines(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto's journey takes him to the islands of Leros, where Mussolini tried to create a fascist utopia during the 1940s, and Patmos, reputedly where the Book of Revelation was written almost 2,000 years ago. Upon reaching Turkey's Turquoise Coast, the architect spots loggerhead turtles(n)

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a lion pride on Kenya's Masai Mara that has reached a dangerous and unusual turning point where the future of the group hangs in the balance. The adult males that had defended the pride have all deserted the group, leaving two remaining adult females to protect their eight cubs from a wide range of threats, including rival prides, herds of buffalo and the constant menace of marauding hyenas. However, the greatest threat of all comes when the lions encounter humans(n)

programme.title: Age of the Image
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox examines how images have become more powerful - and less trusted - than ever before, with the distinction between reality and perception increasingly blurred. He looks at the hyper-real paintings and sculptures of artists Audrey Flack and Ron Mueck, the trickery of Hollywood special effects and image manipulation from Photoshop and green screen to deepfake technology(n)

programme.title: England's Forgotten Queen: The Life and Death of Lady Jane Grey
programme.desc: Three days into her reign, the clock is already ticking for Jane as Mary Tudor, determined to seize power of the throne, raises an army. The Duke of Northumberland is sent from the Tower of London to lead Jane's forces against Mary, and heavily armed ships are deployed to prevent her escaping by sea. With both armies matched in size and firepower, the future of the country hangs in the balance(n)

programme.title: OJ: Made in America
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the rise and fall of OJ Simpson, and what his story reveals about issues of race, celebrity, the media and the justice system in the US. The first episode explores the history of race relations in California and how this may have influenced public attitudes to Simpson's infamous murder trial, as well as exploring his rise to fame in the world of American football(n)

programme.title: Sex and Sensibility: The Allure of Art Nouveau
programme.desc: Stephen Smith examines the influence art nouveau had on British art and design in the early part of the 20th century. He explores the work of illustrator Aubrey Beardsley and architect and designer Charles Rennie Mackintosh, as well as delving into the careers of less celebrated figures, including Mackintosh's wife Margaret MacDonald and artist and craftswoman Mary Watts. Smith also considers how entrepreneur Arthur Liberty helped popularise the art nouveau style through his London department store(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Nicholas Parsons examines the book that gave the world The Owl and the Pussycat to explore the fine line between joy and melancholy in Lear's writing and discover how he pioneered a new kind of poetry(n)

programme.title: Going Going Gone: Nick Broomfield's Disappearing Britain
programme.desc: Documentary in which film-maker Nick Broomfield profiles the Wellington Rooms in Liverpool, and the Coal Exchange in Cardiff, both of which have attained near-iconic status in their respective local areas, but are now being threatened with demolition(n)

programme.title: Age of the Image
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox examines how images have become more powerful - and less trusted - than ever before, with the distinction between reality and perception increasingly blurred. He looks at the hyper-real paintings and sculptures of artists Audrey Flack and Ron Mueck, the trickery of Hollywood special effects and image manipulation from Photoshop and green screen to deepfake technology(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: The rugby legend and wife Maureen travel along the Monmouthshire and Brecon canal, sampling the breath-taking views of the Brecon Beacons. Determined to show that he has mastered the art of narrow-boating, Gareth takes charge of the Bentley of Barges. As a reward for their endeavours, the pair discover how to make gin with ingredients found in the hedgerow. When the canal passes the Glanusk Estate, Gareth seizes the chance to enjoy a spot of fly fishing. Later, in Brecon, they enjoy the performance of a jazz band - a style of music with which the town is synonymous thanks to its annual festival(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain: The Greatest Discoveries
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history during the prehistoric, Roman, Saxon and Tudor eras(n)

programme.title: The Art Mysteries
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak explores the hidden meanings in Seurat's little-known painting Les Poseuses, a depiction of three nudes in an artist's studio which is currently hanging in the Barnes Foundation in Philadelphia. Waldemar tries to ascertain why the artist chose to put an image of one of his more famous works, A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte, in the background(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The 1851 census revealed that Britain had become the world's first urbanised nation, an epochal change that ushered in an idealised vision of the countryside that has been celebrated by some British writers, painters and musicians ever since. Ian Hislop examines this rose-tinted view of the past in works by influential Victorian watercolourist Myles Birket Foster, whose paintings feature thatched cottages and apple-cheeked maids, and author JRR Tolkien, who portrayed the battle between the rural and the industrial in the fantasy world of The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings(n)

programme.title: Lost Sitcoms
programme.desc: A remake of an episode from 1970. Harold is planning on taking a skiing holiday - but he does not want dad Albert there under any circumstances. Jeff Rawle and Ed Coleman star(n)

programme.title: OJ: Made in America
programme.desc: The documentary continues with a look at how OJ Simpson's life in the wealthy, privilege and predominantly white society of Los Angeles contrasted with that of the black community(n)

programme.title: OJ: Made in America
programme.desc: The police discover the bodies at the condo on Bundy Drive and the trial of OJ Simpson for the murders of Nicole Brown Simpson and Ronald Goldman gets under way(n)

programme.title: The Art Mysteries

programme.title: Handmade on the Silk Road
programme.desc: The documentary series follows potter Abdol Reza Aghaei and his father in Meybod, southern Iran, as they make a simple decorated water jug, offering a portrait of two master craftsmen at work. Competing with cheap Chinese imports, the pair sometimes struggle to make a living, but are dedicated to keeping their traditions alive(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: The crafters are tasked to concentrate on the communal areas of the house, having to make from scratch a heavy metal weather vane, a decorative mirror, write, publish and print their own arts and crafts magazine and create a decorative pergola for the front of the house. It remains to be seen whether they can get all the jobs done in a week, just in time to celebrate with a ball and fireworks display. Later, presenters Anita Rani and Keith Brymer Jones assess what the participants have learned about life as a creative crafter in the Victorian era(n)

programme.title: Harry Birrell Presents Films of Love & War (2019)
programme.desc: The life of Harry Birrell, who recorded everything with a wonderfully cinematic eye on thousands of feet of high-quality 16mm film. From commanding a battalion of Gurkhas in the Indian army at the start of the Second World War, to dangerous sorties deep behind enemy lines in Burma at its end, and from the ballroom dances of his youth in the 1930s to teaching his children how to dance the twist in the 1960s, Harry's entertaining and errant adventures are filmed with the intimacy of home movies, but on the scale of Lawrence of Arabia. Narrated by Richard Madden(n)

programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are beside a river. They watch a fisherman make his rod longer and longer and longer. The fisherman casts out the line and then settles down for a nap. Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. programme.desc: After finding a paper cone useful for 'loud talking', Mr Onion arrives at a sleepy castle(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Cloud has been on holiday in space, but when it returns the Clangers notice it is very cold. Mother Clanger is worried when she sees it snowing rather than raining, and she calls for Major Clanger to come and have a look. The Clangers have never seen snow before. Major Clanger finds it very interesting and decides to take a sample to his workshop to analyse, but he's amazed to see that it has disappeared and has been replaced by water. While Small Clanger and Baby Soup Dragon enjoy a snowball fight, Tiny Clanger spots The Cloud shivering and comes up with a clever plan. Will Tiny's plan be enough to stop The Cloud shivering?(n)

programme.desc: Upsy Daisy loves her garden and kisses everything around her - which is when magical things start to happen(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: It has been a week where life in Britain has changed beyond recognition. Schools have shut down, supermarket shelves have emptied, and everyone has been told to stay at home where possible. Meanwhile, the number of deaths is rising and hospitals are preparing for many more Covid-19 patients. Jane Corbin looks at how Britain is coping with its biggest crisis since the Second World War - and asks if the Government has the right strategy to contain the virus(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the fifth and final week of heats. programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie chooses to remain in the UK in a training role as 2 Section prepares to return to Afghanistan, sending a trainee medic in her place. However, when her protegee's confidence slips in the face of the challenges she faces, Georgie feels compelled to join the new recruits in need of her support. However, while protecting a local doctor-turned-politician, she is thrust into an encounter with the militant leader that killed Elvis(n)

programme.title: My Left Nut
programme.desc: Drama about a 15-year-old boy who is horrified to discover a swelling on his testicle. Unprepared to deal with his embarrassing problem and unable to talk to his widowed mother about it, he decides to ignore it, fearing that his friends will make fun of him and that the growth will jeopardise his chances of going out with the girl he has a crush on. Starring Nathan Quinn-O'Rawe(n)

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Anita Rani looks at problems caused by single-use toys, while the residents of the street in Bristol attempt to go plastic free during Christmas - a period that generates a 125,000 ton of packaging. Plus, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall reveals the plastic industry's plans to increase production by 50 per cent before 2040(n)

programme.desc: Stacey Dooley experiences the pressure of life on the frontline of mental health services, spending three weeks working alongside staff at Springfield Hospital in London. During her investigation, she sees first hand how tough it is for medical staff to help young people who are struggling with their mental health, and finds out how hard it is make decisions on what is best for them when their lives are on the line(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: The second series of the spy thriller picks up where the first left off, with MI5 agent Eve and assassin Villanelle left reeling from their final devastating encounter. Villanelle is forced to go on the run, desperate to avoid the wrath of her former employers and plotting to follow her enemy back to London(n)

programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits a fake motorway used for training by emergency services in Gloucestershire, while Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers with a pressing deadline of their own race to refurbish a terraced house in Tylorstown in south Wales, a businesswoman buys a former takeaway to help her business, and a developer got more than he bargains for when he buys a piece of land on the Isle of Sheppey. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as the auction buys are developed(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Insurers find themselves dealing with phantom passengers in a two-vehicle shunt when opportunists attempt to make personal injury claims for people that were not even in the car(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Charlie also travels back in time to explore crime and punishment at Beaumaris Gaol(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi recovers from the attack, but his increasingly desperate persecutor is not giving up easily. Zara treats a notorious alt-right activist guarding an unexpected secret(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A cruelly displayed corpse leads the detectives of an elite murder investigation squad to make questionable decisions as well as prompt concern over a deeper mystery. Crime drama, starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple search for a property in Hampshire, and also visits the Mary Rose Museum to see the treasures uncovered when Henry VIII's warship was raised(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues a coffee table, a set of dining chairs and a glass carboy and demijohn from a recycling centre in Altrincham, enlisting the help of Ollie Allen and Jacqui Joseph(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sam and her brother Chris take part in the culinary contest, with the Essex siblings teaming up with chefs Anna Haugh and Romy Gill. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: My First Home
programme.desc: Vinny Hurrell presents a programme following people who are attempting to get on the property ladder, with interior designer Ian Thompson joining the potential buyers to visit three properties and a dream house for inspiration. Returning to the studio, a virtual makeover of their chosen house is revealed, to give them an idea of what can be done for their budget(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The locals anxiously await the outcome of Whitney's bail hearing, Ian thinks of a way of helping Sharon and Shelley gives Linda a few home truths. Chantelle is having trouble juggling work and home life, so Mitch offers to step in and help. Ben hatches a plan to stay in control ahead of his meeting with Danny - but things turn sour when Callum arrives(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: It has been a week where life in Britain has changed beyond recognition. Schools have shut down, supermarket shelves have emptied, and everyone has been told to stay at home where possible. Meanwhile, the number of deaths is rising and hospitals are preparing for many more Covid-19 patients. Jane Corbin looks at how Britain is coping with its biggest crisis since the Second World War - and asks if the Government has the right strategy to contain the virus(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the fifth and final week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The others must come up with a dish using lemon sole, peppers and Swiss chard, after which two cooks are sent home, leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for 2018 contestant Anthony O'Shaughnessy and former champions Steven Wallis and Shelina Permalloo(n)

programme.title: The King's Hall of Fame
programme.desc: Comedian Jake O'Kane goes on a nostalgic trip back through the archives as he tells the story of the Belfast venue, which is currently being redeveloped into a wellbeing park. He reveals its place in the city's life since 1934, from the annual agricultural show to pop concerts, Ideal Home exhibitions to protest marches, and election counts to boxing(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The annual harvest festival brings the whole village together, but one resident's announcement causes unexpected emotional consequences for the community. Spoof documentary exploring modern life in the Cotswolds, written by and starring siblings Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: In west Wales 19-year-old forex trader Ash persuades YouTube star Gonth to join him in a new business venture. Utilising Ash's knowledge and Gonth's 1.7million fans, they launching a subscription-only webinar offering advice on how to dabble in the forex market. Elsewhere, Twitch streamer Rage Darling wows her fans at Ragecon and world champion Dan Huxtable opens Wales' largest kickboxing club(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A cruelly displayed corpse leads the detectives of an elite murder investigation squad to make questionable decisions as well as prompt concern over a deeper mystery. Crime drama, starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on a rise in attacks on homeless people, while Rav Wilding talks to a former firefighter who has turned her life around after ending up on the streets(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow a four-year project to develop land in Maidstone, Kent, plus properties in Burnley and Meir, Staffordshire(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A chip shop worker tries to claim for a self-inflicted injury, but the truth is caught on CCTV. An impatient motorist claims against a bus company, and fake invoices are exposed(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi waits for vindication as the truth is finally revealed, and Karen helps a long-suffering woman see the true colours in her shady boyfriend(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: Disturbing secrets are exposed about a man struck down on his stag night, and Billie is tempted to act rashly. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Glynis Barber(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple find a characterful country house in the North Wessex Downs on a budget of £775,000, and also pays a visit to Highclere Castle in Wiltshire(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and a team of experts transform a pair of chairs, a collection of metal racks and drawers, and a school desk at Walsall Recycling Centre(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Ellis Barrie and Mike Reid create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by two co-workers who grow their own vegetables. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: After learning of Ben's arrest, Callum is distraught when Kathy reveals Ben was leaving the Prince Albert with another man and makes a difficult decision. Linda is unnerved when Shelley arrives at the Vic and tells Isaac about the landlady's drinking. Tiffany seeks advice from Callum over her problems with Keegan(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Xavier is piecing together the puzzle of Evan's death and getting dangerously close to exposing the truth. How far will Cam go to neutralise him? Ben deals with Roger Ffolkes, who forces him to face some difficult facts, while Jac is keen to impress the Board with a complex and rare surgery that will put them ahead of the competition(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie chooses to remain in the UK in a training role as 2 Section prepares to return to Afghanistan, sending a trainee medic in her place. However, when her protegee's confidence slips in the face of the challenges she faces, Georgie feels compelled to join the new recruits in need of her support. However, while protecting a local doctor-turned-politician, she is thrust into an encounter with the militant leader that killed Elvis(n)

programme.title: My Left Nut
programme.desc: Drama about a 15-year-old boy who is horrified to discover a swelling on his testicle. Unprepared to deal with his embarrassing problem and unable to talk to his widowed mother about it, he decides to ignore it, fearing that his friends will make fun of him and that the growth will jeopardise his chances of going out with the girl he has a crush on. Starring Nathan Quinn-O'Rawe(n)

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Anita Rani looks at problems caused by single-use toys, while the residents of the street in Bristol attempt to go plastic free during Christmas - a period that generates a 125,000 ton of packaging. Plus, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall reveals the plastic industry's plans to increase production by 50 per cent before 2040(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a semi-detached in Sittingbourne, Kent, a cottage in Landrake in Cornwall and a flat in London's Maida Vale(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An opportunist pretending to be a number of different people is caught out, and surveillance footage uncovers a driver fraudulently claiming disability for a serious back injury(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross challenges two teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to scour two antique centres and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in Norfolk. programme.desc: Jimmi's prison ordeal finally comes to an end as The Mill prepares for a special day. Meanwhile, Ruhma is drawn into a shoplifting incident with a unexpected twist(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: Infidelity leads to a man being found dead in bed and a woman being menaced. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to Suffolk to help a couple looking to set down roots in the countryside with a budget of £900,000, and he also meets a dynamic dairy farming family(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the skips at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for items to restore and sell for a profit. Items include a pair of leather wallets and a rocking chair(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Mother and son duo Pat and Tommy compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.desc: The latest reports about Covid-19. Presented by Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Geoff Clark from Sunderland hopes that audio expert Mark Stuckey can restore a beloved 1960s jukebox, and horologist Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a 400-year-old wall clock that has laid broken in a box for years. Elsewhere, Tim Gunn restores a stowaway bike to its former glory(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as the last group of six hopefuls compete in the culinary contest. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The others must come up with a dish using pigeon, beetroot and bacon in the Three Ingredients Challenge, after which two cooks are sent home, leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for three former contestants(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley experiences the pressure of life on the frontline of mental health services, spending three weeks working alongside staff at Springfield Hospital in London. During her investigation, she sees first hand how tough it is for medical staff to help young people who are struggling with their mental health, and finds out how hard it is make decisions on what is best for them when their lives are on the line(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: The second series of the spy thriller picks up where the first left off, with MI5 agent Eve and assassin Villanelle left reeling from their final devastating encounter. Eve finds herself summoned back to London by Carolyn, who offers her a new opportunity. Villanelle is forced to go on the run, desperate to avoid the wrath of her former employers and plotting to follow her enemy back to London(n)

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Equine vet Graham Hunter carries out an endoscopy on one horse and removes a tooth from another - a task that takes a few hours and a lot of patience. At Highland Wildlife Park, vet Jane Harley rasps a Kiang horse's teeth and trims a Markhor's feet. She also wants to take some blood samples from a female bison, but it's quite a challenge for the team as the herd roams freely round the reserve, and the protective and fearsome bull needs to be minded(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Gregor MacLeod and his trusted bakers and cooks share their favourite recipes inspired by countries afar. In Point in Lewis he tries a French pizza called Pissaladière with its salty topping of anchovies and olives. A German man who has made Scotland his home shows Gregor his recipe for rum and raisin cheesecake - just like his grandmother used to make. Gregor takes his inspiration from Spain cooking up light and crispy churros with a rich melted chocolate sauce(n)

programme.title: A' Suathadh Ri Iomadh Rubha
programme.desc: On his death in 2015, Norman Campbell had written published many novels and children's books in Gaelic. He remains one of the 20th century's most respected Gaelic writers. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is an underlying tension between Paddy and Chas. Jacob starts to suspect that Pollard might like Brenda a little more than he is making out, and David gets some good news(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: It becomes clear that Gemma is not coping with motherhood and even the health visitor question her about her state of mind. But later, as the babies' crying escalates, she heads into the yard to calm herself - and realises with horror she is locked out. Alya introduces Yasmeen to a police officer who has information about Geoff - but scared, she refuses to listen and rushed out. David begs Clayton to leave Shona alone, Tyrone gives Arthur a piece of his mind for standing Evelyn up, and Norris reluctantly joins Ken for the Stillwaters' art class(n)

programme.title: St Davids - Britain's Smallest City
programme.desc: The sunny summer season continues, as the Richardsons celebrate a christening at the Cathedral, Jeremy and Stuart have a big performance and Dean Sarah has her portrait painted. Siriol Jenkins narrates(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bernie arrives to find Gemma in such a distressed state, so she agrees to move in and help with the babies - on the condition that she doesn't tell Chesney that Gemma isn't coping. Nick tries to talk to Shona but she refuses to see him, passing on a message to David instead. Arthur apologises to Evelyn and asks her to give him another chance, and Ken and Norris sneak out to the Rovers for a treat(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: As the investigation takes a shocking turn, secrets rise to the surface. When Andrew was living in Edinburgh, a strange turn of events brought him face to face with an old acquaintance, whose deeply corrosive influence becomes clear. Meanwhile, the tragic recent history of Laura's family helps explain the deep bond she and Katy share, as well as the lengths Laura will go to for those she loves. Will her past catch up with her, just as Andrew's did? Thriller, starring Ioan Gruffudd and Joanne Froggatt(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: An interview with Lorenzo Gilyard, who was convicted of killing six people in and around Missouri between 1977 and 1993, and suspected of murdering a further seven more, earning him six life sentences. His prolific spree saw him labelled the Kansas City Strangler, and despite overwhelming DNA evidence pointing to his guilt, he continues to protest his innocence to this day. Here, Piers Morgan sets out to discover what drove a seemingly well-adjusted family man to kill(n)

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of L'Isle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere(n)

programme.title: Dr Ranj: On Call
programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh offers more advice on how to spot, treat and deal with some of the most common illnesses and accidents. As well as going over the latest medical news, the doctor witnesses first-hand what goes on under the knife during a kidney transplant. Kylie Pentelow also meets a young woman who survived terminal cancer following a breakthrough clinical trial(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks
programme.desc: Euston is evacuated during Friday rush hour - leaving passengers there and in Birmingham New Street station stranded, confused and angry. Driver Andy reveals just how close his train came to disaster on the tracks and train dispatcher and former bank manager David imparts his worldly wisdom to a new recruit on his first shift. A train runs a red signal at Birmingham, so engineers have to work through the night to move the stranded locomotive, investigate the cause and get things back up and running before the morning rush(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Kate, the owner of an old-fashioned seaside cafe forms an unlikely friendship with Koji, an African asylum seeker. When Kate finds out that her new acquaintance is a doctor but he is prohibited from working whilst seeking asylum, she hatches a plan that may be mutually beneficial to both involved. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne decides that honesty is required, and hopes that seeking confession with Caroline will appease her guilt and lay to rest the ghosts from her past. However, she has sorely underestimated Lady Brockenhurst, who sets about unearthing the secrets that bind the families together. Hosting a prestigious dinner party, she invites a special guest whose presence reveals a deceit James Trenchard would wish to keep private(n)

programme.title: Culinary Genius
programme.desc: Another nine home cooks are whittled down in three rounds designed to test their culinary skills overseen by Gordon Ramsay. He begins with a test of their knife skills, as they peel, seed and fine-dice a set of cucumbers. The remaining six are then given 10 minutes to joint a duck into six pieces, before the final three use the meat to concoct the tastiest dish they can, with the winner taking home £1,000. Presented by Fern Britton(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie feels guilty, Paddy and Chas worry over the impending visit, and Gabby and Leanna disapprove(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David is devastated about Shona's divorce request but claims it will give him a chance to move on - and when he finds Alina alone in Victoria Garden, it is clear there is a spark between them. News of a family wedding gives Alya the chance she needs to get Yasmeen away from Geoff. The only problem is, they are not invited. Bernie forces Gemma to seek medical help, Evelyn summons Claudia for another makeover and Charles is outraged to discover Ken has smuggled Eccles the dog into his apartment(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Six-part series following Kate, a working-class woman who runs an old-fashioned cafe in a seaside town, as she develops a strong, if sometimes volatile, friendship with an asylum-seeking African doctor, Koji. With Kate and Koji's undercover clinic in full swing, the last thing they need is a visit from Dr Radwan. Medium keeps Koji calm, and Kate cooks up a surefire way to get back at an adversary. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola, Blake Harrison, Barbara Flynn and Victor McGuire(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: An argument in the Rovers turns to blows when David accuses Seb of being jealous of his friendship with Alina - and when Emma realises her boyfriend still has feeling for his ex, she tells him to find somewhere else to stay. Gemma plays down her depression and assures the doctor she is just exhausted, leaving Bernie frustrated by her daughter's refusal to accept help. Alya and Ryan tell Yasmeen they have been invited to Zeedan's wedding in Spain. Now all they have to do is tell Geoff, who is not on the guest list(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Maggie is horrified that her comments about Roxanna have been interpreted by her Polish father Emil as racist, and she is relieved when they understand what she actually did say. Peter has to contend with a campaign for his resignation, as journalists try to dig up his family skeletons. Meanwhile, money lenders Patrick and Phil put the pressure on Alex to repay his debts, and Marcus and Jill's relationship blossoms when he accompanies her to the planning meeting at the village hall for the village celebrations(n)

programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of L'Isle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere(n)

As well as going over the latest medical news, the doctor witnesses first-hand what goes on under the knife during a kidney transplant. Kylie Pentelow also meets a young woman who survived terminal cancer following a breakthrough clinical trial(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star(n)

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks
programme.desc: Euston is evacuated during Friday rush hour - leaving passengers there and in Birmingham New Street station stranded, confused and angry. Driver Andy reveals just how close his train came to disaster on the tracks and train dispatcher and former bank manager David imparts his worldly wisdom to a new recruit on his first shift. A train runs a red signal at Birmingham, so engineers have to work through the night to move the stranded locomotive, investigate the cause and get things back up and running before the morning rush(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Kate, the owner of an old-fashioned seaside cafe forms an unlikely friendship with Koji, an African asylum seeker. When Kate finds out that her new acquaintance is a doctor but he is prohibited from working whilst seeking asylum, she hatches a plan that may be mutually beneficial to both involved. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne decides that honesty is required, and hopes that seeking confession with Caroline will appease her guilt and lay to rest the ghosts from her past. However, she has sorely underestimated Lady Brockenhurst, who sets about unearthing the secrets that bind the families together. Hosting a prestigious dinner party, she invites a special guest whose presence reveals a deceit James Trenchard would wish to keep private(n)

programme.desc: Another nine home cooks are whittled down in three rounds designed to test their culinary skills overseen by Gordon Ramsay. He begins with a test of their knife skills, as they peel, seed and fine-dice a set of cucumbers. The remaining six are then given 10 minutes to joint a duck into six pieces, before the final three use the meat to concoct the tastiest dish they can, with the winner taking home £1,000. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As plans to build a new police station lead Morse to question his place in the force, he investigates the case of Tommy Cork, a boy from a broken home who has disappeared. Meanwhile, the body of disreputable journalist Eric Patterson has been found on railway tracks in mysterious circumstances. He was last seen embroiled in an argument with a council official about a company preparing to redevelop the site of Blenheim Vale, a disused correctional facility for boys. When a petty criminal with a connection to Blenheim absconds from an open prison, it sets off a chain reaction of events that threaten to expose horrors of the past. Did missing Tommy witness something that cost him his life?(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. programme.title: Larry Grayson: Shut That Door!
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the entertainer's rise from poverty to fame, drawing on extracts from an unfinished autobiography. Featuring contributions from friends and colleagues including Michael Barrymore, Janet Street-Porter, Chris Tarrant and Generation Game co-host Isla St Clair(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A vulnerable young man is reported missing by his pub landlord boss. Syndicate 9 suspect Robin McKendrick may have been killed after a photo of him bound and gagged in a car boot appears online. However, the case takes an unusual turn when a body found in a flooded quarry turns out to be a woman's corpse. Meanwhile, battle-scarred from their conflict last year, Scott and Bailey are determined to move forward with honesty. The pair find themselves in front of the promotion board and pass with flying colours, leaving Gill with the task of choosing who stays on as sergeant at the department. Danny Webb joins a cast led by Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe prepares to take up his new post in London, but secretly regrets the decision to leave the Yorkshire Moors. However, the arrival of his replacement PC Wetherby signals it is too late to change his mind. Nurse Carol is administering vaccinations at the school when she notices extensive bruising on a boy's body and suspects he is being abused. The youngster lives with his mother and stepfather, but recently his biological father has been back in touch, trying to see the boy - something the courts will not allow. However, tragedy strikes when conflict grows between the angry father and stepfather - with one of the Aidensfield bobbies caught in the crossfire. Rupert Ward-Lewis joins the cast(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah have an emotional goodbye as he prepares to fly out to Spain to be with Annie. Tina goes shopping for a wedding dress, and Rachel goes into labour(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is excited about the birth of his first grandson. Chris tells Kathy how grateful he is for her help with Rachel, who decides to name her baby Joseph(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek does not like the way Norris is always calling on Angela at work. Don is dismayed when Josie returns his door key, and Martin confesses to Gail that he was unfaithful(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rodney feels the Rovers needs livening up. Gail is stunned when Audrey tells her she knows all about Martin's affair, and Billy Featherstone demands Steve pays his gambling debt(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe prepares to take up his new post in London, but secretly regrets the decision to leave the Yorkshire Moors.
